bull_thesis: AAUC is a signed, friendly, all-cash merger-arb: Zijin Gold International agreed (definitive arrangement, 2026-01-26) to buy Allied Gold at C$44.00/share cash, ~C$5.5B, a 27% premium. At USD/CAD ~1.4146 the deal value is ~$31.08 USD vs a live $25.64 — a ~21% gross spread on a deal just 37 days from a HARD, mutual-agreement-only outside date of 2026-07-29. The runway is almost fully cleared: Investment Canada Act approval is DONE (completing the Canadian review), ECOWAS and COMESA competition clearances are DONE, and African host-country approvals are obtained or in advanced stages. The ONLY load-bearing remaining condition is China's NDRC outbound-investment approval. If NDRC clears before Jul-29 — the parties' base case, and the market's (the stock held near deal value at C$37.31 even on the delay news) — the spread converges to ~$31 USD for +21%. This is a board-recommended, all-cash deal with no financing-out, no CVR, no stock-ratio risk, into a record-high gold-price backdrop that strengthens Zijin's strategic motive to own the ounces. The wide spread is paying you for a single, identifiable sovereign-approval binary, not for a broken or undated process.
bear_thesis: The entire residual risk is one sovereign, non-economic gatekeeper: China's NDRC, which has already DELAYED approval (the reason May→July extension happened), explicitly questioning (1) the 27% premium Zijin is paying — Beijing scrutinizing outbound capital outflow — and (2) Mali political risk, where ~half of Allied's output sits and where the military junta has arrested foreign mining executives and forcibly renegotiated contracts. This is the worst kind of arb condition: third-party, single-binary, and driven by capital-control/geopolitical judgment rather than a clearable checklist. The outside date is mutually-hard ('any further extension requires the parties' mutual agreement') — if NDRC isn't done by Jul-29, an NDRC-blocked Zijin can let the deal lapse. On a break, the premium evaporates: the stock derates toward its standalone, Mali-discounted value (~undisturbed C$34-35 less a risk haircut), i.e., the low-to-mid $20s USD. Mali concentration is the single point of failure that simultaneously drives the discount AND NDRC's hesitation — correlated risk. You are short a sovereign-rejection put for a 21% upside against a ~10% downside, and the spread is wide precisely because that put is live.
sop_bull: Event-win value = the contractual cash consideration. C$44.00/share x USD/CAD 0.7065 (1/1.4146, Jun-2026) = ~$31.08 USD per share at close. No discounting needed beyond a small time-value/odds haircut to the 2026-07-29 outside date; the dossier target of $31.54 (using a slightly stronger ~1.395 CAD) brackets the same figure. Bull SoP ~ $31.00-31.54 USD = the deal value received in cash on completion.
sop_bear: Break floor = standalone gold-miner value, derived from the undisturbed pre-deal price (the C$44 was a 27% premium to the 30-day undisturbed average, implying undisturbed ~C$34-35 ≈ ~$24-25 USD), then haircut for Mali single-jurisdiction risk (~50% of output) but offset upward by record-high gold prices that lift the underlying ounce value. The market's own read on the delay (held at C$37.31 ≈ ~$26.4 USD) sits above the break floor, so $23.00 USD is a conservative break floor — undisturbed USD-equivalent with a Mali risk-off haircut. Downside is bounded; a break strips the premium, it does not impair the gold ounces, and there is no going-concern/covenant/dilution risk.
sop_breakdown: Probability-weighted of the two event outcomes. Assign ~65% to a clean NDRC clearance and close at the cash target ($31.08) and ~35% to an NDRC rejection / Jul-29 lapse to the standalone break floor ($23.00): 0.65 x 31.08 + 0.35 x 23.00 = $28.25; trimming the close-probability for the live, unresolved NDRC overhang and time-value to the outside date yields a base-case ~$27.4 USD — essentially the live price, i.e., the market is pricing ~50-55% close odds at this spread. Bull leg (deal): ~$31.08. Bear leg (break): ~$23.00. Underlying is a real cash-generative gold miner into record bullion, so the floor is hard, not theoretical.
risk_reward: Upside-to-target +$5.44 (+21%, $25.64 -> $31.08) vs downside-to-floor -$2.64 (-10%, $25.64 -> $23.00). Raw asymmetry ~2.06x; on the dossier's $31.54/$23.00 marks ~2.78x. A defined-risk, fat-spread sovereign-binary arb: the wide spread compensates for live NDRC-rejection probability, and the floor caps the loss near 10%.
catalyst_summary: Signed all-cash C$44 Zijin takeover, last gate = China NDRC approval before a hard 2026-07-29 outside date; live $25.64 vs ~$31 USD cash target with a ~$23 standalone floor — defined-risk ~2x merger-arb, gated on one sovereign clearance.
dated_milestone: 2026-07-29 mutually-hard outside date for deal completion (extended once from ~May-2026); the single outstanding condition is China NDRC outbound-investment approval. Canada/ICA, ECOWAS, COMESA, and African host-country approvals already obtained.
forcing_function: The 2026-07-29 outside date — 'any further extension requires the parties' mutual agreement.' Either NDRC clears and the deal closes at C$44 cash (re-rate to ~$31 USD), or it lapses/breaks and the stock derates to its ~$23 standalone floor. The NDRC decision is the trigger; the outside date is the wall that forces resolution.
consensus_delta: This is not a mispriced-narrative story so much as a binary-odds disagreement. The ~21% gross spread, 37 days from a hard outside date, implies the market is pricing only ~50-55% odds of a clean close — extreme skepticism that NDRC clears, anchored on the visible delay and Mali headlines. The bull view is that NDRC's concerns (premium + Mali) are negotiating/process friction on a deal Zijin WANTS (strategic gold ounces into a record bullion market) and that all non-China approvals are already secured, making a clearance-by-Jul-29 more likely than a coin flip. If true, the market is over-discounting a sovereign clearance that ultimately rubber-stamps a strategically-motivated, fully-financed cash deal. The risk to that view is genuine: NDRC is non-economic and capital-control-driven, so 'Zijin wants it' does not guarantee Beijing approves the outflow at a 27% premium for a half-Mali asset.
valley_of_death: The window IS the valley — there is no slow burn, just a single 37-day binary. Near-term path risk: (1) further NDRC silence into July keeps the spread wide and pins the stock; (2) any fresh Mali deterioration (junta action, attack, contract renegotiation) gives NDRC fresh cause to reject and pressures both the deal odds and the standalone floor; (3) headline-driven gap risk on any NDRC leak. There is no cash-burn hump, maturity wall, or dilution flagged — the company is cash-generative at record gold prices, so the floor is structural, not eroding. The only thing that 'decimates' value before the catalyst is an NDRC NO or a Jul-29 lapse, which takes the stock straight to the ~$23 floor (-10%).
positioning_washout: Already partially washed out — the 6.5% drop to C$37.31 on the Jun-2 NDRC-delay news flushed some merger-arb holders and re-widened the spread, which is what creates the entry. The remaining holder base is arb desks underwriting the NDRC binary; a further negative NDRC headline could force another mechanical arb unwind toward the floor (the residual washout risk), while a clearance headline snaps the spread shut. No yield/passive base to force-liquidate; this is a pure event book.
moderator_conclusion: AAUC is a textbook PENDING_HARD defined-risk merger-arb and exactly the setup this funnel is built to size INTO, not cap. The deal is signed, friendly, all-cash (C$44), board-recommended, with NO financing-out and NO CVR games; every approval EXCEPT China NDRC is already secured; and there is a hard, mutual-extension-only outside date of 2026-07-29 just 37 days out. Live $25.64 vs a ~$31 USD cash target with a real, gold-backed ~$23 standalone floor gives ~2x+ asymmetry with a capped ~10% downside. The reason I hold conviction at 4 rather than 5 is honest, not reflexive: the single load-bearing condition is a SOVEREIGN, non-economic gatekeeper (NDRC) that has ALREADY delayed the deal over the premium and Mali risk — a fatter, less-controllable binary than a routine antitrust clearance, with Mali concentration as a correlated single-point-of-failure. That is a real-but-priced risk, not a broken catalyst, so it earns an AGGRESSIVE-ENTRY A with conviction 4, sized for the binary. value_conviction is also 4: the margin of safety is the verified ~$23 break floor (hard, gold-ounce-backed, no solvency/dilution overhang) capping the loss near 10% against +21% upside — that defined downside, not a franchise moat, is what backstops the capital. Forcing function: NDRC decision by the Jul-29 wall. Position it as a sovereign-binary arb with a known floor, not a compounder.
interrogator_dossier:
# AAUC — Allied Gold Corp. | Interrogator Dossier (Event-Driven Special Situation)

**Catalyst class:** Merger-arb / foreign-regulator close. **Super-cluster:** Deal-completion. **As of:** 2026-06-22. Live $25.64 (Jun-18 close) / $25.65 pre-mkt Jun-22 - Deal terms C$44.00 cash - Target ~ $31.08 USD (C$44 @ USD/CAD 1.4146) - Floor $23.00 - Hard date 2026-07-29 (37-42d).

This is NOT a quality/compounding thesis. AAUC is a single-asset-concentrated African gold miner being taken out for cash; the thesis is the SPREAD between $25.64 and the C$44 deal value, gated on the last remaining condition — China NDRC outbound-investment approval — firing before the July 29 outside date. The franchise is irrelevant; the catalyst carries the trade.

## 1. THE EVENT — REAL, DATED, BINDING?
REAL & SIGNED. On 2026-01-26 Allied Gold entered a definitive arrangement agreement to be acquired by Zijin Gold International (HK-listed gold arm of China's Zijin Mining) at C$44.00/share cash, ~C$5.5B equity, a friendly board-recommended all-cash offer — 27% premium to the undisturbed 30-day average. Binding plan-of-arrangement, not an LOI; court/shareholder approvals are non-issues for a friendly recommended cash deal; gating items are regulatory. DATED: Outside Date extended to 2026-07-29 (from ~May-2026), and per the 2026-05-29 release 'any further extension of the Outside Date will require the parties' mutual agreement' — a real hard wall, the forcing function. BINDING on ONE remaining condition: approvals SECURED = Investment Canada Act (completes Canadian review), ECOWAS competition, COMESA competition, African host-country approvals obtained/advanced. Single load-bearing outstanding condition = China NDRC outbound-investment approval.

## 2. THE LOAD-BEARING RISK — NDRC
The entire residual risk collapses to one binary: does Beijing's NDRC clear Zijin's outbound capital? Reporting (InvestingNews/NAI500/Ecofin, 2026-06-02) is explicit that NDRC DELAYED approval, questioning (a) the 27% premium Zijin is paying (outbound capital-outflow scrutiny) and (b) Mali political risk — Mali = ~half of Allied's output, junta has arrested foreign mining execs and forcibly renegotiated contracts. A third-party, single-binary, sovereign-discretion condition with non-economic motives — the worst kind for an arb. The May->July extension was driven by exactly this delay.

## 3. THE FLOOR
On the 2026-06-02 delay news AAUC fell 6.5% to C$37.31 (~$26.4 USD) — market did NOT mark to a break floor; base case still 'closes.' Break floor = standalone-miner value: undisturbed ~C$34-35 (C$44 = 27% premium to 30-day avg) ~ $24-25 USD, haircut for Mali risk, offset up by record gold prices. $23.00 USD floor is defensible and NOT zero: a break strips the premium, not the ounces. No going-concern, no covenant, no ATM/dilution flagged; cash-generative at current gold.

## 4. ASYMMETRY
Target ~$31.08 (+21%) vs floor $23.00 (-10%); raw R:R ~2.06-2.78x. Wide ~21% gross spread 37d from a hard outside date = market pricing non-trivial NDRC-fail probability. That is the source of edge and the reason it is not a layup.

## 5. RED/GREEN FLAGS
RED: NDRC sovereign non-economic gatekeeper actively flagging premium + Mali; Mali single-jurisdiction concentration (~50% output) drives both the discount and NDRC's hesitation (correlated); mutual-agreement-only outside date could lapse if NDRC isn't done by Jul-29. GREEN: friendly board-recommended ALL-CASH definitive agreement (no financing-out/CVR/stock-ratio); every non-China approval already in hand; record gold lifts floor + Zijin's motive; market held near deal value through the delay.

## 6. CREDIBILITY
Event maximally credible as to existence/terms (signed, friendly, cash, dated) but elevated single-condition completion risk (NDRC sovereign discretion over a half-Mali asset at 27% premium). Genuine PENDING_HARD dated catalyst inside the window with a defined floor; the binary is fatter than a routine arb. Moat/secular N/A (gold miner).

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE | MOAT: NONE | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: none
skeptic_kill_fact: I tried to kill this on three vectors and each held against a primary source. (1) CATALYST FIRED/SLIPPED? No — the deal is live, unbroken, and dated: Allied's own 2026-06-10 GlobeNewswire release describes 'Normal Course Progress with Zijin Gold Arrangement' (deal NOT closed and NOT broken as of mid-June), and the 2026-05-29 release (also filed as a 6-K) fixes the hard outside date at 2026-07-29 with the exact load-bearing language 'Any further extension of the Outside Date will require the parties' mutual agreement.' (2) TARGET REAL? Yes — C$44.00/share all-cash is the contractual consideration in a signed plan of arrangement (Jan-26 agreement, shareholders approved 2026-03-31); the target is the cash you receive, not a model. (3) FLOOR REAL? Yes — a break strips the 27% premium but not the gold ounces; no financing-out, no CVR, no going-concern/ATM flagged, so the standalone-miner floor is structural. The single residual binary (China NDRC outbound-investment approval, delayed over premium + Mali risk) is correctly identified as the ONLY outstanding gate and is a real-but-PRICED sovereign-discretion risk, not a disqualifier. The wide ~21% spread is the market paying for that live binary, exactly as the dossier claims. No hidden trading-through-terms, no second-request surprise, no fabricated floor.

catalyst.dated_milestone: 2026-07-29 mutually-hard outside date for deal completion (extended once from ~May-2026); the single outstanding condition is China NDRC outbound-investment approval. Canada/ICA, ECOWAS, COMESA, and African host-country approvals already obtained.
catalyst.binding_reason: Signed friendly all-cash C$44 Zijin takeover, every approval secured EXCEPT one sovereign gate (China NDRC), against a mutual-extension-only hard 2026-07-29 outside date 37 days out; ~21% gross spread to ~$31 USD with a hard gold-ounce-backed ~$23 floor (-10%), ~2x defined-risk. Skeptic CONFIRMED, cap 4 — pinned AT the cap: the single load-bearing binary is a non-economic capital-control gatekeeper that has already delayed once over the premium + Mali risk, a fatter, less-controllable binary than a routine clearance.
